meta.defaultTitle: Thibault M. E. Randrasana — International Trade & Public Policy Analyst
meta.defaultDescription: Analyst in international trade and public policy. Madagascar and the Indian Ocean. Impact studies for public institutions and donors, plus operational experience in liner shipping.

footer.tagline: Analyst in international trade and public policy. Madagascar and the Indian Ocean.

timeline.title: Career timeline
timeline.instructions: Filter by nature or by area. Select a point to expand the full record.

caseStudies.title: Support to the Ministry of Industrialisation and Trade for the diagnostic study on opportunities offered by the AfCFTA for the textile and clothing sector in Madagascar
caseStudies.financement: European Union, via the Support Programme for Export Development and Regional Integration in Madagascar
caseStudies.role: Support to a senior consultant: desk research, analysis, deliverable production.
caseStudies.perimetre: African market under the AfCFTA regime, applied to the development of Madagascar's textile-clothing sector.
caseStudies.livrables: Study report of approximately 50 pages; presentation materials for national consultations.
caseStudies.resultat: Final closing presentation to the Presidency of the Republic of Madagascar.

caseStudies.title: Maritime connectivity and island economies — academic research and operational experience

recherche.title: Research
recherche.text: Master's thesis, 120 pages: "The interdependence of shipping lines and island nations — the cases of Madagascar and Mauritius". Mixed methodology: interviews, data analysis, literature review. Leads to recommendations for Madagascar's logistics chain.

terrain.title: Field experience
terrain.text: Commercial Liner EASTMED, Mediterranean Shipping Company (2025 – current).
bullets: Pricing and capacity allocation on the EASTMED and North Africa trades
Portfolio of 50+ international clients, tenders and one-off requests, handled daily
Tracking a service of ~400 weekly TEU transiting through Le Havre, 3 to 4 calls tracked
Origin ports: Fos, Le Havre, Montoir, Bordeaux, Brest, Dunkirk, Antwerp, Rotterdam, Hamburg, Barcelona, Genoa
Destinations: Eastern Mediterranean basin and North Africa

caseStudies.perimetre: EASTMED and North Africa trades; portfolio of 50+ international clients.
caseStudies.livrables: Pricing and capacity allocation; responses to tenders and one-off requests, handled daily.
caseStudies.resultat: Tracking a service of ~400 weekly TEU transiting through Le Havre (3 to 4 calls tracked).

caseStudies.shortTitle: Economic diplomacy — WTC Antananarivo × WTC Ras Al Khaimah
caseStudies.title: World Trade Center Antananarivo × World Trade Center Ras Al Khaimah
caseStudies.commanditaire: World Trade Center Antananarivo and World Trade Center Ras Al Khaimah (common owner)
caseStudies.perimetre: Opening the business club to both WTCs and promoting both destinations to business circles. Stakeholders: the global WTC network, Madagascar's call-centre sector, government trade organisations, business-support institutions.
caseStudies.livrables: Representation of both WTCs at the Global Forum — approximately 500 participants, around a hundred WTCs represented.
caseStudies.resultat: Project interrupted due to the geopolitical context in Madagascar.

caseStudies.title: Entrepreneurship
caseStudies.note: Presented as an operational complement, not as the core offering.

ploutos.title: Ploutos (2021–2025)
ploutos.livrables: One MVP delivered and one product video mockup. Testing limited to a close circle, no waitlist.
ploutos.resultat: Term sheet for €2M signed at pre-seed with a lead investor (Indo-Malagasy entrepreneur); release conditional on prototype delivery, not reached. Shortlisted for Forbes 30 Under 30 Europe, participation in InnoStartup.
ploutos.lesson: The investment commitment created an expectation that removed any margin for error in the development plan; the schedule allowed for no failure scenario.

recherche.title: Research
recherche.subtitle: The interdependence of shipping lines and island nations — the cases of Madagascar and Mauritius
recherche.methodology: Mixed methodology: interviews, data analysis, literature review. Leads to recommendations for Madagascar's logistics chain.

editorial.title: Editorial production
editorial.intro: Capacity for regular, public writing on Madagascar-related topics, treated as a professional skill.

items.role: Editor-in-chief and columnist — Journal de l'Étudiant (2023–2024)
items.text: Malagasy online outlet with approximately 50,000 followers across all social platforms combined [TO VERIFY]. Author of the weekly column "La Société c'est toi", devoted to Malagasy society — best engagement per post on the outlet [TO VERIFY].
Led a team of about ten columnists.

editorial.verificationNote: [TO VERIFY before publishing: the 50,000-follower figure and the engagement ranking of the column. If either cannot be documented, remove that figure and keep a qualitative statement without a metric.]

education.title: Education
items: Master in Purchasing and Supply Chain Management — INSEEC, Omnes Education
Bachelor in Business Management — CREA Geneva, Omnes Education — valedictorian, class of 2024
Economic development and policy in developing countries · public policy analysis · data analysis for management — London School of Economics and Political Science

engagements.title: Commitments
items: Vice-president, MIRAY association (2020 – present) — material support to families affected by food insecurity in southern Madagascar
Madagascar national basketball team, U16–U18 (2015–2016) — point guard and shooting guard
